steps.title: Spot the degree signal
steps.content: The word more directly modifies deep, showing that deep expresses a greater degree or intensity of seclusion.

steps.title: Match the meaning to the signal
steps.content:
The underlined word "deep" must mean a heightened degree of seclusion — the cliffs evoke "more" of it. Substitute each choice:
  • Choice A — Tough: difficult or hard — a tough seclusion describes hardship, not the intensified remoteness the poem conveys. ✗
  • Choice B — Hideous: ugly or repulsive — nothing in the poem frames the seclusion as ugly; the tone is contemplative, not repelled. ✗
  • Choice C — Complicated: complex or involved — seclusion described as complicated shifts meaning to difficulty, not to degree of depth. ✗
  • Choice D — Intense: extreme in degree — "more intense seclusion" captures the heightened sense of remoteness and quiet the speaker feels. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
